title: tui/event.rs: sealed_deliver calls decrypt_and_display then immediately drops result if not mls_active
description: event.rs SEALED_DELIVER handler: (1) unseals the HPKE outer layer, (2) then checks 'if !state.mls_active { return Ok(()); }', (3) then calls decrypt_and_display with the unsealed plaintext. The MLS-active check happens AFTER the HPKE unseal, meaning HPKE work is done then immediately discarded if MLS is not active. The guard should be before the unseal. More importantly, decrypt_and_display is called with empty from_pub_id string (see nie-f0pz.6), which is not explained by any comment. A reader following the code flow has to understand both the sealed-deliver protocol and MLS to know why this is structured this way.
